title: dianthus caryophyllus var. orophilus
intro: Dianthus caryophyllus var. orophilus, also known as the alpine sweet william, is a variety of the carnation plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Dianthus caryophyllus var. orophilus include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
